_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that families come in many different shapes and that the roles people play in families can change over time.

_source.w: We are learning that families come in many different shapes and that the roles people play in families can change over time.
y: Understanding different family types helps us respect and include everyone.
Seeing how families have changed over time helps us understand our own family better.
Learning about family roles shows us that there is no single right way to be a family.
eg: Listing the different types of families we know about, like big families, small families, and blended families.
Comparing what grandparents did at home when they were young with what our family does now.
Talking about how roles in families have changed — like more dads cooking dinner than in the past.
Reading a story about a family that looks different from ours and discussing what we noticed.
Drawing pictures of families from long ago and families today to spot the differences.
Discussing how Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander kinship connects people in special ways.
